data.title: Correction: Coca-Cola Europacific Partners' FY24 Adjusted Profit, Revenue Rise
data.description:
(Corrects headline and lead to reflect the reported figures and updates to add the adjusted comparable earnings)

Coca-Cola Europacific Partners said Friday reported profit and revenue for 2024 declined year over year, while adjusted figures rose.

Reported profit attributable to shareholders for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 1.42 billion euros, compared with 1.67 billion euros earlier. Reported EPS moved to 3.08 euros from 3.63 euros.

On an adjusted comparable basis, profit was 1.82 billion euros and EPS was 3.96 euros, compared with the year-ago period's 1.71 billion euros and 3.73 euros, respectively.

The bottling company's reported revenue was 20.44 billion euros, compared with 18.30 billion euros a year ago. On an adjusted comparable basis, revenue edged up to 20.71 billion euros from 20.05 billion euros.

Coca-Cola Europacific Partners maintained its 2025 guidance in line with its mid-term strategic objectives with revenue expected to rise 4% and operating profit expected to increase by 7%.

data.title: SIM Technology Group's January Revenue Jumps 25%
data.description:
SIM Technology Group saw a 25% rise in total revenue to HK$42.3 million in January from HK$33.8 million a year prior, a Friday filing with the Hong Kong bourse said.

The mobile communications company's revenue from its core business stood at HK$38.7 million for the month, up 29% from HK$30 million in January 2024.

data.title: First day of trading on the Düsseldorf Stock Exchange
data.description:
Opporisch Aktiengesellschaft

by Mathias Schmid

Business news for the stock market

Opporisch Aktiengesellschaft: First day of trading on the Düsseldorf Stock Exchange

Frankfurt am Main (pta000/14.02.2025/11:50 UTC+1)

As of today, February 14, 2025, Opporisch Aktiengesellschaft is listed on the general open market of the Düsseldorf Stock Exchange. The share capital of Opporisch AG amounts to €500,000.00 and is divided into 500,000 bearer shares (no-par-value shares) with a notional value of €1.00 per share.

As a listed SPAC-type company, Opporisch Aktiengesellschaft offers an efficient platform for rapid access to the German capital market. The advantages of a transaction with a well-capitalized shell company are an increased level of awareness and a broader structure of opportunities for raising capital. An interested listing candidate can be brought into Opporisch AG by means of a contribution in kind; this enables a "reverse IPO" to be implemented quickly and in a targeted manner.

Further documents, such as the articles of association of Opporisch Aktiengesellschaft, can be found on the company's website at https://opporisch.de/.

Concord Capital AG, as the capital market partner, advised on the listing on the Düsseldorf Stock Exchange and ensures compliance with the follow-up obligations in the ongoing listing.

Together with the capital market partner, professional support of the IPO candidates is ensured in the context of the contribution in kind and covers all areas, such as support in the preparation of the expert opinion by an auditor, legal advice, investor relations, capital market transactions and corporate actions supported by banks

(end)

Emitter:Opporisch AktiengesellschaftMünchener Straße 1060329 Frankfurt am MainGermany

data.title: Indian Equities Extend Losses on Trump's Reciprocal Tariffs Threat
data.description:
Indian equity markets closed lower for the eighth consecutive session on Friday, weighed down by new tariff threats from the US.

US President Donald Trump vowed to impose reciprocal tariffs on every country that taxes US imports. Analysts said these potential duties could further drive foreign investments away from India.

At the close, the BSE Sensex declined 199.76 points, or 0.3%, to 75,939.21, while the NSE Nifty 50 dropped 102.15 points, or 0.4%, to 22,929.25.

Adani Ports & Special Economic Zone topped the laggards on Friday, with shares slumping 4%, followed by Bharat Electronics and Adani Enterprises .

Conversely, Britannia Industries , ICICI Bank , and Nestle India were the top gainers.

In corporate news, Glenmark Pharmaceuticals Inc., USA, a unit of Glenmark Pharmaceuticals , launched its Latanoprost Ophthalmic Solution, 0.005%, according to a Friday filing with the Indian stock exchanges. The product is indicated for the treatment of glaucoma and ocular hypertension.

Meanwhile, HDFC Life Insurance received board nod to raise up to 10 billion Indian rupees through the allotment of non-convertible bonds on a private placement basis on Friday, according to a same-day filing with the Indian bourses.

data.title: Skyworth Group Flags 35% Plunge in 2024 Profit
data.description:
Skyworth Group expects to log a 35% decrease in its profit for 2024 compared with that of 2023, a Friday bourse filing said.

The electronics company attributed the expected decrease to the provision of impairment for the group's properties and other inventories.

data.title: Pan Asia Data Names VP
data.description:
Pan Asia Data has appointed Li Yunjiu as its vice president and an executive director effective Friday, a same-day bourse filing said.

Li has served as a vice president at Xindongsen Holdings since January 2024, according to the manufacturer of liquid and powder coatings.

data.title: US 10-Year Treasury Yield Steady at 4.53%, 2-Year Rate Slips 1.8 Basis Points to 4.3% Early Friday
Jupiter Green Investment Trust Plans Reconstruction, Winding-up Scheme
data.description:
Jupiter Green Investment Trust said Friday its board proposed a scheme of reconstruction to wind up the company.

The investment trust offered shareholders the option to roll their investment into units in the Jupiter Ecology Fund or receive cash equal to the company's net asset value.

Shareholders will vote on the proposed liquidation and scheme of reconstruction at two general meetings scheduled for March 5 and March 14.

data.title: Indian shares fall as US tariff worries persist
data.description:
India's benchmark indexes fell on Friday as investors fretted over the implications of U.S. President Donald Trump's plans to impose reciprocal tariffs, which analysts said could hurt the country the most among its Asian peers.

Trump is planning to slap reciprocal tariffs on every country taxing U.S. imports. The imposition of these duties, however, is likely to be delayed, helping global stocks stage a relief rally.

Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi met Trump on Thursday and offered to talk about easing tariffs, buying more U.S. oil and gas, combat aircraft and trade concessions.

However, that did little to calm markets back home. The Nifty 50 lost 0.44% to 22,929.25 while the BSE Sensex fell 0.26% to 75,939.21.

"Indian markets have seen sharper losses on the day due to the high tariff differential with the U.S., compared to most other Asian economies," said Narendra Solanki, head of research at Anand Rathi.

Analysts said the potential consequences of U.S. tariffs on the Indian rupee and U.S. interest rates could trigger further foreign outflows, hurting domestic equities.

Both the Nifty and Sensex have lost about 2.5% this week, their worst in 2025 so far.

In contrast to domestic equities, other Asian markets rose, with the MSCI Asia ex-Japan index (.MIAPJ0000PUS) gaining 1.1% on the day.

All 13 major domestic sectors were down. Drug makers , which have a significant revenue exposure to the United States, fell the most with a 3% decline.

The small-caps and mid-caps tumbled 3.6% and 2.4%, respectively, extending their downward trend on concerns over slowing corporate earnings and stretched valuations.

The small-cap index is currently down about 21.6% from its record closing high on December 11 confirming bear territory. The mid-caps are 18.4% below their peak closing level on September 24.

data.title: U.K. Defense Export Orders Reached Highest Level in a Decade in 2023 — Market Talk
data.description: The value of defense export orders won by the U.K., home to primes such as BAE Systems and Rolls-Royce, jumped 49% in 2023 compared with the previous year, reaching its highest level in a decade, according to official figures released Friday. The U.K. Department for Business and Trade says that British defense companies won defense contracts worth 14.5 billion pounds in 2023, an increase of 4.8 billion pounds in nominal prices compared with 2022. Aerospace products and services exports accounted for 56% of total value in 2023, it notes. The rise was caused by significant increases in exports to Europe, particularly Poland. The Middle East, which had been on average the largest market for U.K. defense export orders over the five years to 2022, was overtaken by Europe in 2023, the data show. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

data.title: Russia's Central Bank On Hold, But Mulls Future Rate Rise
data.description:
By Paul Hannon

Russia's central bank Friday left its key interest rate unchanged for a second straight meeting, but warned it may raise borrowing costs soon if a surge in prices driven in large part by the invasion of Ukraine doesn't abate.

The Bank of Russia left its key rate at 21%, the highest level recorded since the full-scale invasion of Ukraine in early 2022. It said that demand for loans had eased and savings had increased in response to high interest rates.

But it also noted that the Russian economy has grown faster than it had expected, and that households expect high rates of inflation to continue.

"The Bank of Russia will assess the need for a key rate increase at its upcoming meeting, taking into consideration the speed and sustainability of the inflation slowdown," the central bank said in a statement.

Write to Paul Hannon at paul.hannon@wsj.com

data.title: SocGen's Overnight Economic News Summary
data.description:
Societe Generale in its early Friday economic news summary pointed out:

— bid with before Munich security conference, natural gas retreats below EUR50/Mwh. Euro Stoxx -0.2% after the United States imposes customized reciprocal tariffs from April 1. 30-year U.S. Treasury refunding tailed 1.1bps, strong non-dealer demand of 83.7%. — Japanese investors bought net 1,75 billion yen in international bonds last week, the most since last September. Sold net 1,27 billion yen international stocks.

— Day ahead: European Union, U.S., Ukraine, Russia officials attend Munich conference. U.S. retail sales, SocGen forecasts -0.3% month over month, excluding autos 0.2%, 'control' 0.3%. Industrial production, SocGen forecasts: -0.3%. Federal Reserve speaker Logan. Eurozone Q4 gross domestic product second reading. Romania's central bank forecast to stay on hold.

data.title: Palm rises on lower production, logs fourth weekly gain
data.description:
Malaysian palm oil futures ended higher on Friday and logged a fourth straight weekly gain, as lower stocks supported the market despite demand concerns and uncertainties surrounding Indonesia's biodiesel mandate.

The benchmark palm oil contract for April delivery on the Bursa Malaysia Derivatives Exchange gained 41 ringgit, or 0.9%, to 4,595 ringgit ($1,036.78) a metric ton at the close. The contract rose 2.02% this week.

The market adjusted slightly on demand concerns though lower end stocks kept prices supported, said Paramalingam Supramaniam, director at Selangor-based brokerage Pelindung Bestari.

"Uncertainties surrounding Indonesia's biodiesel mandate is also keeping trading cautious."

Indonesia expects its B40 biodiesel programme, aimed at reducing its reliance on imported diesel fuel, will reach full implementation next month after delays at the start of the year, energy ministry official Eniya Listiani Dewi said.

Dalian's most-active soyoil contract (DBYcv1) rose 0.13%, while its palm oil contract climbed 0.38%. Soyoil prices on the Chicago Board of Trade were up 0.93%.

Palm oil tracks price movements of rival edible oils, as it competes for a share of the global vegetable oils market.

Cargo surveyors are expected to release their estimates on Malaysian palm oil exports for February 1-15 on Saturday.

Oil prices rose and were poised to end three weeks of decline, buoyed by rising fuel demand and expectations that U.S. plans for global reciprocal tariffs would not come into effect until April, giving more time to avoid a trade war. O/R

Stronger crude oil futures make palm a more attractive option for biodiesel feedstock.

The ringgit , palm's currency of trade, strengthened 0.47% against the dollar, making the commodity more expensive for buyers holding foreign currencies.

More than 60% of Argentina's soy crop is in average-to-excellent condition after recent rains refreshed the fields, which had been battered by hot, dry weather, the Buenos Aires Grain Exchange said on Thursday.

($1 = 4.4320 ringgit)

data.title: Hermes Plays in a League of Its Own — Market Talk
data.description: Hermes seems to be playing in a league of its own, Citi analysts Thomas Chauvet and Mahesh Mohankumar say in a note. The French luxury group reported industry-beating growth during the fourth quarter, they say. The group booked quarterly revenue of 3.96 billion euros, nearly 18% higher at constant exchange rates compared with the prior-year period and beating analysts' forecasts of 3.73 billion euros. Hermes is well positioned thanks to continued brand heat, supply and demand imbalances and pricing power, the analysts say. Shares are up 0.7% at 2,837 euros. (andrea.figueras@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

1045 GMT - Hermes seems to be playing in a league of its own, Citi analysts Thomas Chauvet and Mahesh Mohankumar say in a note. The French luxury group reported industry-beating growth during the fourth quarter, they say. The group booked quarterly revenue of 3.96 billion euros, nearly 18% higher at constant exchange rates compared with the prior-year period and beating analysts' forecasts of 3.73 billion euros. Hermes is well positioned thanks to continued brand heat, supply and demand imbalances and pricing power, the analysts say. Shares are up 0.7% at 2,837 euros. (andrea.figueras@wsj.com)

1032 GMT - The value of defense export orders won by the U.K., home to primes such as BAE Systems and Rolls-Royce, jumped 49% in 2023 compared with the previous year, reaching its highest level in a decade, according to official figures released Friday. The U.K. Department for Business and Trade says that British defense companies won defense contracts worth 14.5 billion pounds in 2023, an increase of 4.8 billion pounds in nominal prices compared with 2022. Aerospace products and services exports accounted for 56% of total value in 2023, it notes. The rise was caused by significant increases in exports to Europe, particularly Poland. The Middle East, which had been on average the largest market for U.K. defense export orders over the five years to 2022, was overtaken by Europe in 2023, the data show. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

1041 GMT - RWE's management has begun to address investors' concerns and the process of rebuilding confidence is under way, UBS analysts write in a note. Shares in the German utility have underperformed the sector on concerns about renewable-energy growth in the U.S. and higher-for-longer interest rates, the analysts write. The company's outlook is positive given it has diversified renewable and thermal assets across different countries, they say. RWE's current business plan, fromNovember 2023, is out of date however and its 55 billion euros capital-expenditure plan through to 2030 is optimistic and likely to be lower, they add. Shares trade flat at 28.38 euros but are down 11% over the past three months.(adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

1025 GMT - Moncler's performance has few implications for how peers in the sector are doing, Berenberg analysts Nick Anderson and Harrison Woodin-Lygo say in a research note. This is because the company's strong results were the consequence of internal efforts to drive profitability, they say. In difficult economic periods, clients migrate to brands at front of mind, the analysts note, echoing Moncler's management views. Moncler's client loyalty was especially noticeable in China, where a strong rebound reflected the strength of the business model and continued investment, Berenberg adds. Shares are down 0.5% at 67.86 euros. (maitane.sardon@wsj.com)

1017 GMT - Segro's earnings were consistent with forecasts, Shore Capital analyst Andrew Saunders says in a note. The real-estate investment trust remains committed to one of the biggest development programs in the sector with significant potential for value creation, he says. It has 422 million pounds of potential new rent from the current landbank including a pipeline of data centers close to key European cities, he says. And it has consistently delivered like-for-like rental growth, with 2024 being its best year as it added 91 million pounds of new rent, he says. "Segro offers attractive earnings growth along with additional scope to upgrade our conservative forecasts," Saunders says. Shares are up 0.1% at 723 pence. (anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com)

1007 GMT - Safran's fourth-quarter revenue grew 19% and edged slightly above consensus expectations driven by the French aerospace-industry supplier's aerospace propulsion unit, RBC Capital Markets analyst Ken Herbert says in a research note. The company's financial guidance for 2025 includes the expectation of 15% to 20% growth in Leap engine deliveries this year, while Safran excludes any potential impacts from new tariffs. Supply-chain productivity will be the key determining factor for Safran's outlook for this year, the analyst says. The company raised its guidance for adjusted earnings, which offers some incremental upside, but RBC says the targets remain conservative. Shares trade 0.77% higher to 247.70 euros. (pierre.bertrand@wsj.com)

1001 GMT - Moncler's rebound in China wasn't by chance but a result of strong brand building and marketing efforts, Berenberg analysts Nick Anderson and Harrison Woodin-Lygo say in a research note. The maker of high-end puffer jackets exceeded consensus expectations for organic revenue growth by more than 8%. The beat was entirely driven by the Moncler brand's retail operations, which benefited from a resurgent Chinese cluster, the analysts note. The strong results help alleviate concerns on the company's exposure to China and its dependence on outerwear, they say. Shares are down 0.6% at 67.78 euros. (maitane.sardon@wsj.com)

0953 GMT - Rheinmetall's midterm guidance and estimates are conservative as they only reflect the order pipeline based on current defense spending pledges, despite clear signals of an impending spending surge, HSBC analysts Dario Dickmann and Christoph Dolleschal say. This means Rheinmetall should experience continued upgrades of midterm estimates and targets in the years to come, they add. The analysts don't think a ceasefire in the Ukraine-Russia war would materially change the arms maker's midterm picture, because there are no indications that Ukraine-related sales or EU defense spending would decrease. HSBC raised its share-price target for Rheinmetall to 1,000 euros from 700 euros. Shares are up 5.15% at 796 euros. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

0950 GMT - Hermes International ended 2024 on a high note, with sales growth accelerating in the final quarter of the year, which sets a high bar for 2025, Stifel's Rogerio Fujimori says in a note. The French luxury group posted an 18% increase in sales at constant currency in the fourth quarter, accelerating from growth of 11% in the third and significantly ahead of consensus expectations, the Stifel analyst says. "The only negatives we see for 2025 are the high comparison base, further adverse FX hedging impact in [the first half] and additional 350 million-euro taxes in France--hopefully temporary," the analyst says. Shares rise 2.6%. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0948 GMT - Ubisoft Entertainment's confirmation of financial targets is encouraging since its fiscal year closes at the end of next month, UBS analysts write in a research note. The French videogame maker is forecasting net bookings of roughly 1.9 billion euros, break-even non-IFRS operating profit and free cash flow. The analysts attribute confirmation of those targets to revenue that Ubisoft could generate from partnerships as well as from videogame "Assassin's Creed Shadows," for which pre-orders have been solid so far. Ubisoft said pre-orders for the upcoming release are in line with those of "Assassin's Creed Odyssey," the second-most successful entry of the franchise. Ubisoft shares trade 2.4% higher at 11.40 euros. (mauro.orru@wsj.com)

0933 GMT - Singapore's FTSE Straits Times Index closed 0.1% lower at 3877.50, following two consecutive days of gains. Markets are likely digesting the changing U.S. tariff policies as well as the hotter-than-expected inflation data this week. Investors will be eyeing the U.S. retail sales numbers due later today for further clues on the health of the U.S. economy, the Commerzbank Research team wrote in a note. Among the biggest decliners, Singapore Exchange fell 5.8%, DFI Retail Group lost 2.6% and Singtel was 1.5% lower. Banks were mixed, with UOB and OCBC up 1.1% and 0.3%, respectively, while DBS Group shed 0.5%. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

0932 GMT - NatWest's profitability guidance could be unsustainable, Shore Capital analyst Gary Greenwood writes in a research note. The U.K. lender guided for its return on tangible equity--a key measure of profitability--to land in the 15% to 16% range in 2025, and to stay over 15% in 2027. This represents an uplift from its previous guidanceand aligns with consensus expectations.. "While a RoTE of this magnitude may be achievable in the near-term, we worry whether it will being sustainable over the longer-term given competitive pressure and the risk that such a return could attract the wrong sort of political and regulatory attention," Greenwood notes. Shares trade 1.7% lower at 429.4 pence. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

data.title: Bank of Ireland to Trim Interest Rates for Fixed-Term Deposits
data.description:
Bank of Ireland (BIRG.IR) will reduce the interest rate on its 12- and 18-month fixed-term deposits by 0.25% starting Feb. 18, according to a Friday release.

The 12-month fixed-term return will change to 1.99% from the current 2.24%, with an annual equivalent rate, or AER, of 2.00%. The new 18-month fixed-term return will be 3.73% from 4.11%, with an AER of 2.48%.

The Irish lender added that the 3.00% AER for its 12-month regular savings account, as well as the rate for its other savings products, will remain unchanged.

data.title: Why is XRP price up today?
data.description:
XRP price is up today, mirroring upside moves elsewhere in the cryptoсurrency market.

Things to know:

  • XRP’s price has jumped by around 3% on Feb. 11, on course to record its best intraday performance in over a week.

  • The gains appear as chances of an XRP-specific exchange-traded fund (ETF) approval in the US increase.

  • Ripple’s partnership with Unicâmbio supports XRP’s upside.

Increasing XRP ETF approval odds fuel speculation

XRP price trades in a second straight bullish session on the daily chart as analysts see an increasing likelihood of XRP ETF filings receiving a green light from the US Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC).

Key points: 

  • On Feb. 10, Bloomberg ETF analysts Eric Balchunas and James Seyffart included XRP among their list of the most likely crypto ETF filings to receive approval from the SEC.

  • XRP ETFs hold a 65% chance of approval, according to their Monday post on X. 

  • Several asset managers, including CoinShares, Grayscale, 21Shares, Canary, and Bitwise, have already filed applications for XRP spot ETFs.

  • This signals strong institutional interest. 

  • However, the SEC has yet to acknowledge the filings.

XRP ETFs could pull off an impressive performance if approved, and the increasing possibility of this happening has sparked speculation, as evidenced by the altcoin’s derivatives market.

  • Open interest in XRP futures increased by approximately $290 million over the last 24 hours,

  • This indicates that more traders are opening leveraged positions on XRP, likely anticipating big price moves.

  • XRP’s funding rates—periodic payments exchanged between long and short traders in perpetual futures contracts to keep prices aligned with the spot market—turned positive.

  • This shift suggests that traders are paying to take long positions or betting on a price increase. 

Ripple’s partnership with Unicâmbio

The bullishness in XRP price today can also be attributed to Ripple’s latest announcement that it has partnered with Portuguese currency exchange provider Unicâmbio to support instant cross-border payments between Portugal and Brazil.

What you should know:

  • The partnership will leverage Ripple’s payment platforms to make faster and cheaper cross-border settlements.

  • It also strives to increase efficiency in the process.

  • This marks the first Ripple payment solution in Portugal, expanding the company’s footprint in Europe.

“Our partnership with Unicâmbio is a significant milestone in Ripple’s European expansion. Portugal has developed a thriving crypto ecosystem, so we are delighted to open up our payments solution to partners there,” said Cassie Craddock, Managing Director, UK and Europe, at Ripple, adding:

“As the global cross-border payments market continues to expand, more institutions like Unicâmbio are leaning in to benefit from the inherent benefits of blockchain technology.”

Analysts see XRP price rising to $8

In response to the latest recovery in XRP price, Dark Defender, an anonymous crypto analyst, said that the altcoin could hit an intermediate cycle target of $5.85 and a long-term target of $8.76 based on the Elliott Wave Theory in the daily time frame.

“XRP almost finalized the consolidation on the daily Chart, as seen from the RSI lows. Wave 3 is preparing to fire the bullet against the $5.85 parapet. The short-term target is $5.85 and $8.76.”

  • Dark Defender has used this structure to anticipate these targets since July 2023, when the price ranged between $0.40 and $0.50. 

  • Recently, the same analyst projected the XRP price to go as high as $18 this cycle.

Fellow analyst CasiTrades also noted that XRP is in the fourth wave of its intermediate cycle in the hourly timeframe, with an anticipated C wave pushing the price toward $2.92. 

“We are at a decision in the market being at the apex of the consolidation now! A break above would target $2.92 for a potential C wave. Breaking down would target the support I’ve listed.”

As Cointelegraph noted earlier, XRP price recovering above the 50-day SMA at $2.63 would confirm the return of a bull market structure. 

This article does not contain investment advice or recommendations. Every investment and trading move involves risk, and readers should conduct their own research when making a decision.

data.title: HSFO cracks slip back into discounts as rally retreats
data.description:
Refining cracks for 380-cst high sulphur fuel oil (HSFO) have fallen back into discounts by the end of the week, following a few days of short-lived premiums.

Singapore prompt 380-cst HSFO/Brent crack (FO380BRTCKMc1) closed near discounts of 33 cents a barrel on Friday, showed LSEG data, with discounts widening further after the Asia close.

The rally showed signs of stalling in line with earlier market expectations, while backwardation spreads also eased.

Meanwhile, spot trade remained largely thin. As for downstream bunkers, demand continued to be slow in the month, trade sources said.

Sales of marine fuel logged weaker volumes in the first month of 2025, official data showed on Friday, edging higher on both monthly and yearly basis.

Singapore delivered-basis bunker premiums for very low sulphur fuel oil (VLSFO) held near low $10s to cargo quotes this week, compared with high $10s in January, according to sources.

Cargo premiums for VLSFO have softened slightly in the week, while cracks (LFO05SGBRTCMc1) continued to inch lower towards premiums near $11.60 a barrel.

INVENTORY DATA

- ARA fuel oil inventories (STK-FO-ARA) rose 4.6% to 1.39 million tons in the week to Feb. 13, based on data from Dutch consultancy Insights Global.

OTHER NEWS

- Oil prices rose on Friday, poised to end three weeks of decline, buoyed by rising fuel demand and expectations that U.S. plans for global reciprocal tariffs would not come into effect until April, giving more time to avoid a trade war.

- Tightened U.S. sanctions on Moscow have disrupted a roaring trade in discounted Russian oil to China and India, reviving demand for Middle Eastern and African crudes, roiling shipping markets and driving up oil prices.

- India and the U.S. agreed to start talks to clinch an early trade deal and resolve their standoff over tariffs as New Delhi promised to buy more U.S. oil, gas and military equipment and fight illegal immigration.

- Indonesia expects its B40 biodiesel programme, aimed at reducing its reliance on imported diesel fuel, will reach full implementation next month after delays at the start of the year, energy ministry official Eniya Listiani Dewi said on Friday.

WINDOW TRADES

data.title: Reliance Consumer Products Acquires Velvette - Statement
data.description:
Reliance Industries Ltd :

  • RELIANCE CONSUMER PRODUCTS LIMITED ACQUIRES VELVETTE - STATEMENT

data.title: Marui Group's Profit Up Nearly 8% in Fiscal Nine Months
data.description:
Marui Group's profit attributable to owners of the parent rose 7.7% to nearly 19 billion yen in the nine months ended Dec. 31, 2024, from 17.6 billion yen a year ago.

The retailer's earnings per share increased to 101.64 yen from yen 93.23 yen a year earlier, according to a Tokyo Stock Exchange filing on Thursday.

Operating revenue grew 10% to 187.8 billion yen from 170.5 billion yen in the previous year.

Marui Group expects an attributable profit of 26.5 billion yen, EPS of 141.42, net sales of 254.2 billion yen, and a total dividend of 106 yen for the fiscal year ending March 31.
